---
title: "Bug Report"
date: today
params:
  title: "Sparse GRM Incompatible with LOCO GWA Mapping"
  nf_version: ">=24.10.0,<25.0.0"
  executor: "local"
  command: "nextflow run main.nf -profile test"
  error: |
    #Chr 3:
    1175 SNPs on chromosome 3 are included in the analysis.
    Performing REML analysis ...
    13 observations, 2 fixed effect(s), and 2 variance component(s)(including residual variance).
    Prior values of variance components:  0.00000 16.70448
    logL: -18.14486
    Running AI-REML algorithm ...
    Iter.  logL     V(G)     V(e)
    1      -21.35   0.00000  94.15479                (1 component(s) constrained)
    2      -26.52   0.00000  1391.99609              (1 component(s) constrained)
    3      -41.11   0.00000  265874.10680            (1 component(s) constrained)
    4      -69.98   0.00000  9598528154.52585        (1 component(s) constrained)
    5      -127.70  0.00000  12509437511561904128.00 (1 component(s) constrained)
    ...
    8      -935.74  0.00000  5.10e+149               (1 component(s) constrained)
    Error: the information matrix is not invertible.
    An error occurs, please check the options or data
  config: |
    // modules/gcta/perform_gwa/main.nf (before fix)
    // Both inbred and loco modes used the sparse GRM (--grm-sparse)
    // created by --make-bK-sparse in the same process
  context: |
    Root cause: The sparse GRM (created by --make-bK-sparse) zeros out pairwise
    relatedness values below a threshold. LOCO mode computes leave-one-chromosome-out
    kinship as K_(-k) = K_total - (M_k/M) * K_k. When K_total_sparse[i,j] = 0 but
    K_k[i,j] > 0 (computed from raw genotypes), the subtraction produces negative
    off-diagonal kinship values. This makes the covariance matrix non-positive
    semi-definite, causing the AI-REML optimizer to diverge.

    Evidence:
    1. Failures were chromosome-specific (chr III failed, chr II converged) — same
       run, same sparse GRM — pointing to the per-chromosome subtraction step.
    2. Inbred mode (--fastGWA-mlm-exact) worked on the same data — it uses the sparse
       GRM directly without any subtraction.
    3. Switching to the full binary GRM (.grm.bin) for LOCO mode resolved all failures
       across all 9 populations.

    Fix applied in modules/gcta/perform_gwa/main.nf:
    - Inbred (--fastGWA-mlm-exact): continues to create and use sparse GRM via
      --make-bK-sparse / --grm-sparse (no subtraction step involved).
    - LOCO (--mlma-loco): now skips sparse GRM creation entirely and passes the full
      binary GRM from GCTA_MAKE_GRM directly via --grm.
format: gfm
engine: knitr
---

# Bug Report: `r params$title`

**Date:** `r Sys.Date()`
**Nextflow version:** `r params$nf_version`
**Executor:** `r params$executor`

---

## Description

The LOCO (Leave-One-Chromosome-Out) GWA mapping mode failed with AI-REML divergence on specific chromosomes when using a sparse GRM. The sparse GRM approximation (`--make-bK-sparse`) is incompatible with the LOCO per-chromosome kinship subtraction step (`K_(-k) = K_total - (M_k/M) * K_k`), because zeroed-out entries in the sparse total GRM minus nonzero per-chromosome contributions produce negative off-diagonal kinship values, making the covariance matrix non-positive semi-definite.

The symptom was V(e) diverging exponentially (doubling order of magnitude each iteration) until the information matrix became singular. Failures were isolated to individual chromosomes within otherwise successful runs.

## Command Used

```bash
`r params$command`
```

## Error Output

<!-- Paste from .nextflow.log or work/xx/yyyy*/.command.err -->

```
`r params$error`
```

## Config / Profile

<!-- Paste only the relevant sections — process resources, executor settings, param overrides -->

```groovy
`r params$config`
```

## Additional Context

`r params$context`

---

*Generated with the Quarto GitHub Issue Framework. Submit via `gh issue create --title "<title>" --label "bug" --body-file <this_file>.md`*
